items.requirement: A user can open the local app and choose either review question A or B before input is processed.
items.evidence: Local app exposes both review questions and requires Pre-process workflow.

items.requirement: The selected review question is saved into the run manifest, receipts, and summary.
items.evidence: Selected review question is present in receipts and summary framing.

items.requirement: A user can paste redacted text or upload a constructed/deidentified local file and click Pre-process.
items.evidence: Paste and upload paths are represented in the local app proof payload.

items.requirement: A user can run a local CLI command with constructed text input and receive a redaction report plus draft structured episode.
items.evidence: Constructed input path emits a redaction report and prepared draft artifacts.

items.requirement: Residual PII/PHI risk is detected and blocks or quarantines unsafe input.
items.evidence: Residual risk block and quarantine behavior are both checked.

items.requirement: A reviewer-approved structured episode can be saved and used for deterministic analysis.
items.evidence: Approved structured episode can be run through the deterministic analysis endpoint.

items.requirement: Synthetic and constructed demo inputs validate against schemas.
items.evidence: Synthetic case library and constructed demo artifact path validate.

items.requirement: Hidden future facts cannot enter current-time node computation.
items.evidence: Current validation report has zero future-leakage failures.

items.requirement: Every graph node has a NodeAudit or equivalent schema-backed audit object.
items.evidence: Every graph node has a node audit.

items.requirement: Every node audit has dependencies, evidence refs, value, range, median, distribution kind, confidence, method, and sensitivity note.
items.evidence: Node audits have required estimate, evidence, dependency, and sensitivity fields.

items.requirement: The app displays Node Audit Methodology after pre-processing and before processing.
items.evidence: Node Audit Methodology is visible before processing.

items.requirement: A reviewer can choose OK, Adjust, or Re-check selected nodes; adjustments require confirmation before replacing generated methodology.
items.evidence: OK, Adjust, and Re-check selected-node controls are present and traced.

items.requirement: Every ensemble contribution is accepted, rejected, or downgraded with a reason.
items.evidence: Every ensemble contribution has a disposition and reason.

items.requirement: The app displays Ensemble Contributions before Process.
items.evidence: Ensemble Contributions are visible in the pre-process/review flow before Process.

items.requirement: The graph computes final Sentinel posture from normalized typed node values.
items.evidence: Graph posture is computed from deterministic typed fixture runs.

items.requirement: Final posture remains one of the Sentinel posture categories, not a disposition recommendation.
items.evidence: Forbidden disposition-language scan reports zero generated-artifact violations.

items.requirement: The primary result is a clinician-readable governance summary of no more than one or two short paragraphs.
items.evidence: Primary clinician summary is concise and machine-checked.

items.requirement: The primary summary explains what the result means for the selected review question and why, including the main uncertainty drivers.
items.evidence: Summary includes selected-question framing and governance boundary.

items.requirement: A Deeper Dive button exposes node audit tables, ensemble tables, receipts, trace hashes, validation status, structured episode, and raw JSON/Markdown artifacts.
items.evidence: Deeper Dive exposes trace hashes and raw JSON/Markdown receipt links.

items.requirement: JSON receipts include selected review question, redaction, intake, node audit, ensemble, trace, graph, and signature-placeholder fields.
items.evidence: JSON receipts include selected question and required audit fields.

items.requirement: Human-readable receipts explain what was known, what was missing, what would have changed the discussion, what facility-based monitoring or treatment might add, what non-inpatient alternatives might add, commission concerns, omission concerns, therapy-response concerns, and why the graph selected the posture.
items.evidence: Human-readable receipts include summary and deeper-dive sections.

items.requirement: The workbench renders the redacted input, structured episode, node methodology, distributions, range, median, ensemble disagreement, graph posture, clinician summary, deeper-dive artifacts, receipts, and validation status.
items.evidence: Workbench renders summary, methodology, receipts, validation status, and deeper-dive artifact index.

items.requirement: Automated validation reports cover schema validity, future leakage, redaction gating, expected posture agreement on fixtures, omission detection, commission warning detection, therapy-response integration, next-best-information usefulness, node-audit completeness, receipt completeness, workbench completeness, app-flow completeness, summary completeness, and forbidden phrase violations.
items.evidence: Validation report covers schema, leakage, redaction, fixture agreement, detection categories, node audit, receipts, workbench, app flow, summary, and forbidden phrases.

items.requirement: The project documents what is implemented, what is deferred, and what would be required before any real clinical, prospective, production, or live-evidence use.
items.evidence: Status document separates implemented, deferred, required-before-real-use, and not-claimed boundaries.

items.requirement: Full local verification commands pass and git diff --check is clean.
items.evidence: Run the final verification command set after this generated audit is committed; this item is intentionally not inferred from static files.
